plugins.name: Public API Plugin
plugins.description: Public, API-key-authenticated HTTP surface for Peckboard, served as a WASM plugin. Lets external clients list projects, list/create cards, and manage scoped API keys over a /plugin-api/* prefix.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: Diff Viewer
plugins.description: Pick any git repo in the project/session folder, then get a side-by-side viewer/editor for every file that differs from origin/main, including new files and images, served as a WASM plugin.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: Experts
plugins.description: Experts feature for Peckboard (knowledge / question / PM experts, MCP tools, and UI), served as a WASM plugin.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: Session Control
plugins.description: Control another Peckboard session from a session: interrupt, terminate the agent, clear, and send messages/images. Served as a WASM plugin.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: Playwright Tests
plugins.description: LogRocket-style replay of recorded browser test runs: network waterfall with masked request/response detail, console panel, event timeline with rage-click detection, cursor replay with click ripples, session details, and time-scaled playback with inactivity skipping — on a Playwright Tests page below Sessions.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: Chicken Coop
plugins.description: A 3D chicken run visualizing every live session as a bird: a hen per active card (out of the coop while working, pecking on tool activity — peck style varies by tool class — nesting during testing steps, walking home on done/wont_do), a rooster for chat sessions, a barred hen for repeating-task runs, a bantam for temp sessions, and chicks in the parent's palette that follow their parent bird for subagents — a chick runs back to its parent and vanishes when the subagent completes. When a session asks a question, an alert badge pops over its bird — click to answer in a modal with full context. Sidebar page with a live-polled state endpoint; all three.js, no external assets.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: Pre-hatcher
plugins.description: Pre-warms chat messages before they reach the main model: each intercepted message first gets a plain opt-in question card (no AI involved) — declining sends it unchanged; accepting has a temp session on a cheaper model (the provider's cheapest priced one, or the model picked in Settings) gather repository context with the full chat transcript in view, ask a clarifying question when the request is ambiguous, and propose an enriched message delivered only after the user approves it on a second plain question card showing the expanded text (declining delivers the original). The research session runs under a configurable library system prompt (default "fable 5"). Research actions stream into the parked message live. Served as a WASM plugin.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: Nginx Proxy Manager
plugins.description: Manage an Nginx Proxy Manager instance from Peckboard sessions: bridges NPM's built-in MCP server (proxy hosts, redirections, streams, certificates, access lists) as MCP tools — npm_status, npm_list_tools, npm_call, npm_configure. Passthrough surface: the tool catalog is discovered live per API-key scope. Requires the http_request permission (outbound HTTP incl. private/LAN targets) to reach a self-hosted NPM. Served as a WASM plugin.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: Kaiad
plugins.description: Manage a Kaiad control plane from Peckboard sessions: bridges Kaiad's hosted MCP server (services & deployments, builds, registry, agents, operators, incidents) as MCP tools — kaiad_status, kaiad_list_tools, kaiad_call, kaiad_configure. Passthrough surface: the tool catalog is discovered live per credential scope (mcp.read/mcp.write). Requires the http_request permission (outbound HTTP incl. private/LAN targets) to reach a self-hosted Kaiad panel. Served as a WASM plugin.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

plugins.name: SSH Fleet
plugins.description: Hold connection details for many SSH hosts (user+password or user+key) and drive them over MCP tools: register hosts, run commands on one host or across the whole fleet, and read/write/edit remote files over SFTP. Ships a live activity dashboard (searchable host picker, per-host and all-hosts views, live polling). SSH runs through native host functions in Peckboard core, so credentials stay in memory and are never written to disk. Served as a WASM plugin.
plugins.author: PeckBoard

mcp_servers.name: Playwright
mcp_servers.description: Browser automation for agents using Playwright: interact with real web pages through structured accessibility snapshots, no screenshots or vision models required.
mcp_servers.author: Microsoft

mcp_servers.name: Context7
mcp_servers.description: Fetches up-to-date, version-specific documentation and code examples for any library directly from official sources — no hallucinated or outdated APIs.
mcp_servers.author: Upstash
mcp_servers.setup_note: Works without a key at basic rate limits. For higher limits, get a free API key at https://context7.com/dashboard and add the arguments: --api-key YOUR_KEY.

mcp_servers.name: GitHub
mcp_servers.description: GitHub's official MCP server: read repositories, manage issues and pull requests, analyze code, and automate workflows from agent sessions.
mcp_servers.author: GitHub
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a Personal Access Token at https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens/new and set the Authorization header to: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN.

mcp_servers.name: Notion
mcp_servers.description: Official Notion MCP server: agents read, create, and edit Notion pages and databases through the Notion API.
mcp_servers.author: Notion
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create an internal integration at https://www.notion.so/profile/integrations to get a token (starts with ntn_), then connect the integration to the pages or databases the agent should access.

mcp_servers.name: Filesystem
mcp_servers.description: Filesystem operations over MCP: read/write files, create/list/delete directories, move files, search, and file metadata — scoped to the directories you allow.
mcp_servers.author: Anthropic
mcp_servers.setup_note: Replace /path/to/allowed/dir with the absolute path the server may access; add more paths as extra arguments.

mcp_servers.name: Memory (Knowledge Graph)
mcp_servers.description: Persistent agent memory backed by a local knowledge graph — remember entities, relations, and facts across sessions.
mcp_servers.author: Anthropic

mcp_servers.name: Sequential Thinking
mcp_servers.description: Structured problem-solving tool: break complex problems into steps with revision and branching support.
mcp_servers.author: Anthropic

mcp_servers.name: Clear Thought
mcp_servers.description: Advanced reasoning toolkit: one clear_thought tool with 38 operations — mental models, debugging approaches, tree-of-thought, beam search, MCTS, graph-of-thought, decision frameworks, and metacognitive monitoring.
mcp_servers.author: Waldzell AI

mcp_servers.name: Sequential Thinking Tools
mcp_servers.description: Sequential thinking with per-step tool recommendations: breaks problems into thought steps and suggests which available MCP tools to use next, with confidence scores, branching, and revision history.
mcp_servers.author: Scott Spence

mcp_servers.name: Structured Thinking
mcp_servers.description: Mind-map style reasoning with metacognition: capture thoughts with quality scores and stages (problem definition, analysis, ideation), branch parallel reasoning paths, and retrieve past thoughts from short/long-term memory.
mcp_servers.author: Promptly Technologies

mcp_servers.name: MCP Reasoner
mcp_servers.description: Systematic reasoning engine with beam search and Monte Carlo Tree Search over scored thought trees — strong on math, logic puzzles, and problems with uncertain outcomes.
mcp_servers.author: Jacck
mcp_servers.setup_note: Not on npm. Clone https://github.com/Jacck/mcp-reasoner, run npm install (builds dist/ via the prepare script), then replace /path/to/mcp-reasoner with the clone's absolute path.

mcp_servers.name: Postgres MCP Pro
mcp_servers.description: PostgreSQL for agents with index tuning, explain plans, health checks, and safe SQL execution (configurable read/write access).
mcp_servers.author: Crystal DBA
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Set DATABASE_URI to your PostgreSQL connection URI, e.g. postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/dbname. Use --access-mode=restricted for read-only analysis.

mcp_servers.name: Brave Search
mcp_servers.description: Official Brave MCP server: web, local, image, video, and news search with AI-powered summarization via the Brave Search API.
mcp_servers.author: Brave
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get a free API key at https://api-dashboard.search.brave.com/app/keys.

mcp_servers.name: Fetch
mcp_servers.description: Web content fetching: retrieves pages and converts HTML to markdown for easy agent consumption. Respects robots.txt by default.
mcp_servers.author: Anthropic
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv installed.

mcp_servers.name: Atlassian
mcp_servers.description: Atlassian's official Rovo MCP Server: real-time access to Jira, Confluence, Jira Service Management, Bitbucket, and Compass — search, create, and update issues and pages, with existing Atlassian permissions enforced.
mcp_servers.author: Atlassian
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a personal API token at https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens and set the Authorization header to: Basic BASE64_OF_email:api_token (an org admin must enable API token auth for the Rovo MCP Server; service-account API keys use Bearer instead). To sign in with OAuth instead, add the Atlassian (OAuth) entry.

mcp_servers.name: Atlassian (OAuth)
mcp_servers.description: Atlassian's official Rovo MCP Server via OAuth sign-in: Jira, Confluence, Jira Service Management, Bitbucket, and Compass with your existing Atlassian permissions — no API token to create.
mcp_servers.author: Atlassian
mcp_servers.setup_note: Click Sign in with Atlassian to connect with OAuth. For API-token auth use the plain Atlassian entry instead.

mcp_servers.name: Linear
mcp_servers.description: Linear's official MCP server: find, create, and update issues, projects, and comments in your Linear workspace.
mcp_servers.author: Linear
mcp_servers.setup_note: Click Sign in with Linear to connect with OAuth (recommended — nothing to paste). Alternatively create an API key under Settings → Security & Access and set the Authorization header to: Bearer YOUR_KEY.

mcp_servers.name: Datadog
mcp_servers.description: Datadog's official MCP server: query metrics, logs, traces, monitors, dashboards, incidents, and other observability data from agent sessions.
mcp_servers.author: Datadog
mcp_servers.setup_note: Pick your Datadog site in the Region dropdown, then click Sign in with Datadog — the OAuth sign-in follows the selected region. Datadog only completes sign-in for allow-listed redirect URLs: if it fails with 'Mismatching redirect URI', add the callback URL shown in the sign-in panel in Datadog under Organization Settings > Preferences > MCP OAuth Redirect URLs, then retry. Alternatively set the Authorization header to: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN using a Personal or Service Access Token — or replace the header with DD_API_KEY and DD_APPLICATION_KEY rows. Append ?toolsets=all to enable all toolsets.

mcp_servers.name: Slack
mcp_servers.description: Slack's official MCP server: search channels and messages, read threads, send messages, and work with canvases in your Slack workspace.
mcp_servers.author: Slack

oauth.scopes: search:read.public search:read.private search:read.mpim search:read.im search:read.files search:read.users files:read channels:history groups:history im:history mpim:history users:read chat:write

mcp_servers.setup_note: Slack does not allow automatic client registration: create your own Slack app at https://api.slack.com/apps, add the callback URL shown in the sign-in panel to the app's OAuth settings, then click Sign in and enter the app's client id and secret. On Enterprise Grid with SSO, pin the workspace by adding an extra sign-in parameter team=YOUR_WORKSPACE_ID (the T… id) — Slack then routes the sign-in through your org's IdP. Alternatively paste a user OAuth token (xoxp-…) as the Authorization header: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN.

mcp_servers.name: Git
mcp_servers.description: Git repository tools over MCP: read, search, and manipulate local repositories — status, diff, log, branch, commit, and more.
mcp_servers.author: Anthropic
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Optionally restrict access with: --repository /path/to/repo.

mcp_servers.name: Time
mcp_servers.description: Time and timezone conversion tools: current time in any IANA timezone and conversions between zones.
mcp_servers.author: Anthropic
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv.

mcp_servers.name: SQLite
mcp_servers.description: Query and analyze a local SQLite database: run SQL, inspect schemas, create tables, and capture business insights.
mcp_servers.author: Anthropic
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Replace --db-path with the absolute path to your SQLite database file.

mcp_servers.name: Sentry
mcp_servers.description: Sentry's official MCP server: query issues, errors, and performance data, search across projects, and invoke Seer for root-cause analysis.
mcp_servers.author: Sentry
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth — click Sign in with Sentry to connect your org.

mcp_servers.name: GitLab
mcp_servers.description: GitLab's official MCP server: read projects, issues, and merge requests, and work with GitLab Duo context from agent sessions.
mcp_servers.author: GitLab
mcp_servers.setup_note: URL shown is GitLab.com; for self-managed use https://YOUR_GITLAB_HOST/api/v4/mcp (GitLab 18.2+). Authenticates with OAuth 2.1 dynamic client registration.

mcp_servers.name: Chrome DevTools
mcp_servers.description: Google's official Chrome DevTools MCP server: control and inspect a live Chrome browser — performance traces, network requests, console, screenshots, and page automation.
mcp_servers.author: Google
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Chrome installed on the same machine.

mcp_servers.name: CircleCI
mcp_servers.description: CircleCI's official MCP server: find and fix build failures, retrieve pipeline and job logs, and analyze flaky tests.
mcp_servers.author: CircleCI
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a personal API token at https://app.circleci.com/settings/user/tokens.

mcp_servers.name: Semgrep
mcp_servers.description: Semgrep's official MCP server: scan code for security vulnerabilities and quality issues with Semgrep rules, straight from agent sessions.
mcp_servers.author: Semgrep
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv and the semgrep CLI on PATH (e.g. pip install semgrep). SEMGREP_APP_TOKEN is optional — set it to use Semgrep AppSec Platform rules (get one at https://semgrep.dev/orgs/-/settings/tokens).

mcp_servers.name: SonarQube
mcp_servers.description: SonarSource's official MCP server: analyze code quality and security — issues, quality gates, metrics, and languages from SonarQube Server or Cloud.
mcp_servers.author: SonarSource
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Docker. Generate a token in SonarQube under My Account → Security; SONARQUBE_ORG is your SonarQube Cloud organization key (for SonarQube Server set SONARQUBE_URL instead).

mcp_servers.name: E2B Code Interpreter
mcp_servers.description: E2B's official MCP server: run code in secure cloud sandboxes — execute Python and shell in isolated VMs for data analysis and code interpretation.
mcp_servers.author: E2B
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API key at https://e2b.dev/dashboard.

mcp_servers.name: Postman
mcp_servers.description: Postman's official MCP server: manage workspaces, collections, environments, and APIs in your Postman team.
mcp_servers.author: Postman
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create an API key at https://postman.co/settings/me/api-keys and set the Authorization header to: Bearer YOUR_KEY.

mcp_servers.name: DeepWiki
mcp_servers.description: Cognition's DeepWiki MCP server: ask questions about any public GitHub repository and read AI-generated architecture docs — no authentication required.
mcp_servers.author: Cognition

mcp_servers.name: Microsoft Learn
mcp_servers.description: Microsoft's official Learn MCP server: search and fetch trusted, up-to-date Microsoft/Azure documentation and code samples — no authentication required.
mcp_servers.author: Microsoft

mcp_servers.name: AWS Knowledge
mcp_servers.description: AWS's official Knowledge MCP server: search and read AWS documentation, API references, What's New posts, and architectural guidance — no AWS account required.
mcp_servers.author: AWS

mcp_servers.name: Azure
mcp_servers.description: Microsoft's official Azure MCP server: work with Azure resources — storage, Cosmos DB, Azure CLI, monitor logs, and dozens of other services.
mcp_servers.author: Microsoft
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Node.js 22+. Uses your local Azure credentials — sign in first with: az login.

mcp_servers.name: AWS API
mcp_servers.description: AWS's official API MCP server: call AWS CLI commands from agents with validation and suggestions — manage and inspect resources across all AWS services.
mcp_servers.author: AWS
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv and AWS credentials configured locally (aws configure / SSO / env vars).

mcp_servers.name: Terraform
mcp_servers.description: HashiCorp's official Terraform MCP server: query the Terraform Registry for provider docs and modules to power accurate IaC generation.
mcp_servers.author: HashiCorp
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Docker.

mcp_servers.name: Pulumi
mcp_servers.description: Pulumi's official MCP server: query the Pulumi Registry, get resource schemas, and run Pulumi CLI operations like preview and up from agent sessions.
mcp_servers.author: Pulumi
mcp_servers.setup_note: Pulumi CLI operations use your local Pulumi login and cloud credentials.

mcp_servers.name: Kubernetes
mcp_servers.description: Kubernetes cluster management over MCP: pods, deployments, services, logs, exec, port-forward, and Helm — driven by kubectl against your current context.
mcp_servers.author: Flux159
mcp_servers.setup_note: Uses your local kubeconfig (current kubectl context). Requires kubectl; Helm optional.

mcp_servers.name: Cloudflare Docs
mcp_servers.description: Cloudflare's official documentation MCP server: search up-to-date Cloudflare docs (Workers, R2, KV, DNS, and more) — no authentication required.
mcp_servers.author: Cloudflare

mcp_servers.name: Cloudflare Observability
mcp_servers.description: Cloudflare's official observability MCP server: debug Workers applications — logs, analytics, errors, and usage data for your Cloudflare account.
mcp_servers.author: Cloudflare
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Cloudflare account on first connect.

mcp_servers.name: Globalping
mcp_servers.description: jsDelivr's official Globalping MCP server: run ping, traceroute, mtr, DNS, and HTTP measurements from thousands of probes worldwide.
mcp_servers.author: jsDelivr
mcp_servers.setup_note: Works anonymously at basic rate limits; sign in with OAuth for higher limits.

mcp_servers.name: Vercel
mcp_servers.description: Vercel's official MCP server: manage projects and deployments, analyze build logs, and search Vercel documentation.
mcp_servers.author: Vercel
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Vercel account on first connect.

mcp_servers.name: Netlify
mcp_servers.description: Netlify's official MCP server: create, deploy, and manage sites, forms, environment variables, and access controls.
mcp_servers.author: Netlify
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a personal access token at https://app.netlify.com/user/applications (or leave empty to use your local netlify login).

mcp_servers.name: Render
mcp_servers.description: Render's official MCP server: create and manage services, databases, and environment variables, and pull logs and metrics.
mcp_servers.author: Render
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create an API key at https://dashboard.render.com/settings#api-keys and set the Authorization header to: Bearer YOUR_KEY.

mcp_servers.name: Heroku
mcp_servers.description: Heroku's official MCP server: manage apps, addons, dynos, logs, and Postgres databases on the Heroku platform.
mcp_servers.author: Heroku
mcp_servers.setup_note: Generate an API key with: heroku authorizations:create, or from Account Settings → Applications.

mcp_servers.name: Grafana
mcp_servers.description: Grafana Labs' official MCP server: search dashboards, query Prometheus/Loki datasources, and manage incidents and alerts in your Grafana instance.
mcp_servers.author: Grafana Labs
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Docker. Set GRAFANA_URL to your instance (e.g. https://myorg.grafana.net) and create a service account token under Administration → Service accounts.

mcp_servers.name: PagerDuty
mcp_servers.description: PagerDuty's official MCP server: query and manage incidents, services, schedules, escalation policies, and on-call data.
mcp_servers.author: PagerDuty
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Create an API user token in PagerDuty under My Profile → User Settings → API Access.

mcp_servers.name: Prometheus
mcp_servers.description: Query Prometheus over MCP: run PromQL queries, explore metrics and metadata, and check targets against your Prometheus server.
mcp_servers.author: Pavel Shklovsky
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Set PROMETHEUS_URL to your server (e.g. http://localhost:9090); optional PROMETHEUS_USERNAME/PROMETHEUS_PASSWORD for basic auth.

mcp_servers.name: Browserbase
mcp_servers.description: Browserbase's official MCP server: cloud browser automation with Stagehand — navigate, act, extract, and screenshot on headless browsers at scale.
mcp_servers.author: Browserbase
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get API key and project ID at https://www.browserbase.com/settings. Stagehand also needs a model key (GEMINI_API_KEY by default; other providers configurable).

mcp_servers.name: Firecrawl
mcp_servers.description: Firecrawl's official MCP server: web scraping, crawling, search, and structured extraction — JavaScript rendering, batch scraping, and deep research.
mcp_servers.author: Firecrawl
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API key at https://firecrawl.dev/app/api-keys.

mcp_servers.name: Bright Data
mcp_servers.description: Bright Data's official MCP server: web search, scraping, and structured data feeds that bypass geo-restrictions and bot detection at scale.
mcp_servers.author: Bright Data
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API token at https://brightdata.com/cp/setting/users (free tier available).

mcp_servers.name: Apify
mcp_servers.description: Apify's official MCP server: run any of 6,000+ Apify Actors — scrapers and extractors for websites, social media, maps, e-commerce, and more.
mcp_servers.author: Apify
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API token at https://console.apify.com/settings/integrations.

mcp_servers.name: Hyperbrowser
mcp_servers.description: Hyperbrowser's official MCP server: cloud headless browsers for scraping, structured extraction, crawling, and browser-use agents.
mcp_servers.author: Hyperbrowser
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API key at https://app.hyperbrowser.ai.

mcp_servers.name: Exa Search
mcp_servers.description: Exa's official MCP server: AI-native web search — real-time results, code context, company research, crawling, and deep research tools.
mcp_servers.author: Exa
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API key at https://dashboard.exa.ai/api-keys.

mcp_servers.name: Tavily
mcp_servers.description: Tavily's official MCP server: real-time web search, content extraction, site crawling, and mapping built for AI agents.
mcp_servers.author: Tavily
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API key at https://app.tavily.com (free tier available).

mcp_servers.name: Perplexity
mcp_servers.description: Perplexity's official MCP server: live web-wide research via the Sonar API — ask questions and get grounded, up-to-date answers with citations.
mcp_servers.author: Perplexity
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API key at https://www.perplexity.ai/settings/api.

mcp_servers.name: Kagi Search
mcp_servers.description: Kagi's official MCP server: high-quality ad-free web search and page summarization via the Kagi API.
mcp_servers.author: Kagi
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Get an API key at https://kagi.com/settings?p=api (search API access currently requires an invite).

mcp_servers.name: DuckDuckGo Search
mcp_servers.description: Web search through DuckDuckGo with result formatting and content fetching — no API key required.
mcp_servers.author: Nick Clyde
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv.

mcp_servers.name: MongoDB
mcp_servers.description: MongoDB's official MCP server: query and manage MongoDB databases and Atlas clusters — find, aggregate, schema inspection, and Atlas administration.
mcp_servers.author: MongoDB
mcp_servers.setup_note: Set MDB_MCP_CONNECTION_STRING to your connection string (mongodb://... or mongodb+srv://...); for Atlas administration set MDB_MCP_API_CLIENT_ID/SECRET service-account credentials instead.

mcp_servers.name: Supabase
mcp_servers.description: Supabase's official MCP server: manage projects, run SQL, apply migrations, query logs, and generate TypeScript types for your Supabase databases.
mcp_servers.author: Supabase
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a personal access token at https://supabase.com/dashboard/account/tokens. Recommended extra args: --read-only --project-ref=YOUR_PROJECT_REF.

mcp_servers.name: Neon
mcp_servers.description: Neon's official MCP server: manage serverless Postgres — create projects and branches, run SQL, and handle migrations in natural language.
mcp_servers.author: Neon
mcp_servers.setup_note: Replace YOUR_NEON_API_KEY with an API key from https://console.neon.tech/app/settings/api-keys.

mcp_servers.name: ClickHouse
mcp_servers.description: ClickHouse's official MCP server: run SELECT queries and explore databases and tables on your ClickHouse cluster or ClickHouse Cloud.
mcp_servers.author: ClickHouse
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Try it keyless against the public SQL playground: host sql-clickhouse.clickhouse.com, user demo, empty password.

mcp_servers.name: MotherDuck / DuckDB
mcp_servers.description: MotherDuck's official MCP server: run SQL analytics on DuckDB — query local database files or MotherDuck cloud databases.
mcp_servers.author: MotherDuck
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Get a token at https://app.motherduck.com/settings/tokens — or query a local file instead with: --db-path /path/to/local.db (drop the token argument).

mcp_servers.name: Elasticsearch
mcp_servers.description: Elastic's official MCP server: explore indices, run queries and aggregations, and inspect mappings on your Elasticsearch cluster.
mcp_servers.author: Elastic
mcp_servers.setup_note: Set ES_URL to your cluster endpoint and create an API key in Kibana under Stack Management → API keys.

mcp_servers.name: Redis
mcp_servers.description: Redis' official MCP server: manage and search data in Redis — strings, hashes, lists, sets, streams, JSON, and vector indexes.
mcp_servers.author: Redis
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Replace the --url value with your Redis connection URI (rediss://user:pass@host:port for TLS/Cloud).

mcp_servers.name: Chroma
mcp_servers.description: Chroma's official MCP server: vector database operations — create collections, add documents, and run semantic search, with local or cloud backends.
mcp_servers.author: Chroma
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Defaults to an ephemeral in-memory client; use --client-type persistent --data-dir /path for local persistence, or cloud/http client flags for hosted Chroma.

mcp_servers.name: Qdrant
mcp_servers.description: Qdrant's official MCP server: a semantic memory layer — store and retrieve documents by vector similarity on your Qdrant instance.
mcp_servers.author: Qdrant
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Set QDRANT_URL (e.g. http://localhost:6333 or your cloud URL), an API key for Qdrant Cloud, and the collection to use.

mcp_servers.name: Pinecone
mcp_servers.description: Pinecone's official MCP server: search docs, create indexes, upsert and query vectors, and rerank results on your Pinecone projects.
mcp_servers.author: Pinecone
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an API key at https://app.pinecone.io.

mcp_servers.name: Neo4j
mcp_servers.description: Neo4j's official MCP server: query graph databases with Cypher — schema introspection plus read and write queries against your Neo4j instance.
mcp_servers.author: Neo4j
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Set NEO4J_URI (e.g. neo4j+s://xxx.databases.neo4j.io or bolt://localhost:7687) and credentials.

mcp_servers.name: MySQL
mcp_servers.description: MySQL access over MCP: list tables, read data, and execute SQL queries with configurable access controls.
mcp_servers.author: DesignComputer
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Use a restricted MySQL user for safety.

mcp_servers.name: Airtable
mcp_servers.description: Read and write Airtable bases over MCP: inspect schemas, list records, and create, update, and delete rows.
mcp_servers.author: Adam Jones
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a personal access token at https://airtable.com/create/tokens with scopes schema.bases:read and data.records:read (add write scopes as needed).

mcp_servers.name: Convex
mcp_servers.description: Convex's official MCP server: introspect tables and functions, run queries, and read logs against your Convex deployments.
mcp_servers.author: Convex
mcp_servers.setup_note: Run from within a Convex project directory (uses your local convex login).

mcp_servers.name: Asana
mcp_servers.description: Asana's official MCP server: search, create, and update tasks, projects, goals, and comments in your Asana workspace.
mcp_servers.author: Asana
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Asana account on first connect.

mcp_servers.name: monday.com
mcp_servers.description: monday.com's official MCP server: manage boards, items, updates, and docs on your monday.com account.
mcp_servers.author: monday.com
mcp_servers.setup_note: Replace YOUR_MONDAY_API_TOKEN with a token from monday.com → Avatar → Developers → My access tokens.

mcp_servers.name: ClickUp
mcp_servers.description: ClickUp's official MCP server: manage tasks, lists, spaces, docs, and comments in your ClickUp workspace.
mcp_servers.author: ClickUp
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your ClickUp workspace on first connect.

mcp_servers.name: Figma
mcp_servers.description: Figma's official MCP server: give agents design context — read files, frames, components, and variables, and generate code from selections.
mcp_servers.author: Figma
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Figma account on first connect. (The Figma desktop app also serves a local Dev Mode variant at http://127.0.0.1:3845/mcp.)

mcp_servers.name: Canva
mcp_servers.description: Canva's official MCP server: create, search, and export Canva designs, and autofill templates from agent sessions.
mcp_servers.author: Canva
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Canva account on first connect.

mcp_servers.name: Microsoft 365
mcp_servers.description: Microsoft 365 over MCP via the Graph API: Outlook mail and calendar, OneDrive files, Excel workbooks, To Do, Planner, and Teams.
mcp_servers.author: Softeria
mcp_servers.setup_note: Sign in via the built-in login tool (device-code flow) on first use. Add --org-mode for work/school accounts with Teams and SharePoint.

mcp_servers.name: Google Workspace
mcp_servers.description: Google Workspace over MCP: Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Slides, Tasks, Forms, and Chat with full OAuth support.
mcp_servers.author: Taylor Wilsdon
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv and a Google Cloud OAuth client (Desktop type) with the Workspace APIs you need enabled; sign-in happens in the browser on first use.

mcp_servers.name: Obsidian
mcp_servers.description: Work with your Obsidian vault over MCP: list, read, search, and edit notes through the Local REST API community plugin.
mcp_servers.author: Markus Pfundstein
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv and the 'Local REST API' community plugin enabled in Obsidian — copy its API key here.

mcp_servers.name: MarkItDown
mcp_servers.description: Microsoft's official MarkItDown MCP server: convert files to Markdown — PDF, Word, PowerPoint, Excel, images (OCR), audio, HTML, and more.
mcp_servers.author: Microsoft
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv.

mcp_servers.name: Excel
mcp_servers.description: Create and edit Excel workbooks over MCP: read and write data, formulas, formatting, charts, and pivot tables — no Microsoft Excel install needed.
mcp_servers.author: Haris Musa
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv.

mcp_servers.name: Zapier
mcp_servers.description: Zapier's official MCP server: call actions across 8,000+ connected apps — Gmail, Sheets, Slack, CRMs, and more — from one MCP endpoint.
mcp_servers.author: Zapier
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create an MCP server at https://mcp.zapier.com, choose the actions to expose, and set the Authorization header to: Bearer YOUR_ZAPIER_MCP_TOKEN.

mcp_servers.name: HubSpot
mcp_servers.description: HubSpot's official MCP server: work with CRM objects — contacts, companies, deals, tickets, associations, and properties.
mcp_servers.author: HubSpot
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a private app in HubSpot (Settings → Integrations → Private Apps) with CRM scopes and paste its access token.

mcp_servers.name: Intercom
mcp_servers.description: Intercom's official MCP server: search and manage conversations, contacts, tickets, and help-center content in your Intercom workspace.
mcp_servers.author: Intercom
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Intercom workspace on first connect.

mcp_servers.name: Stripe
mcp_servers.description: Stripe's official MCP server: create customers, products, payment links, and invoices, inspect subscriptions and disputes, and search the Stripe docs.
mcp_servers.author: Stripe
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a restricted API key (rk_…) at https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys — the key's permissions control which tools are enabled.

mcp_servers.name: Stripe (hosted)
mcp_servers.description: Stripe's hosted remote MCP server: the same account tools as the local @stripe/mcp package, served from mcp.stripe.com with OAuth sign-in — no API key or local Node needed.
mcp_servers.author: Stripe
mcp_servers.setup_note: Click Sign in with Stripe to connect with OAuth (recommended). Alternatively set the Authorization header to: Bearer rk_… using a restricted API key from https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys.

mcp_servers.name: PayPal
mcp_servers.description: PayPal's official MCP server: create and manage invoices, orders, payments, subscriptions, and disputes on your PayPal business account.
mcp_servers.author: PayPal
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your PayPal account on first connect (sandbox variant: https://mcp.sandbox.paypal.com/mcp).

mcp_servers.name: Square
mcp_servers.description: Square's official MCP server: work with the Square ecosystem — payments, customers, catalog, orders, inventory, and team management.
mcp_servers.author: Square
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Square account on first connect.

mcp_servers.name: Shopify Dev
mcp_servers.description: Shopify's official Dev MCP server: search Shopify docs, explore Admin GraphQL schemas, and get Polaris and Functions guidance — no store credentials needed.
mcp_servers.author: Shopify

mcp_servers.name: Plaid
mcp_servers.description: Plaid's official MCP server: inspect and debug your Plaid integration — items, institutions, errors, and usage from the Plaid dashboard.
mcp_servers.author: Plaid
mcp_servers.setup_note: Set the Authorization header to: Bearer YOUR_CLIENT_ID:YOUR_SECRET using the API keys from https://dashboard.plaid.com/developers/keys.

mcp_servers.name: Xero
mcp_servers.description: Xero's official MCP server: accounting operations — contacts, invoices, quotes, payments, bank transactions, and reports on your Xero organisation.
mcp_servers.author: Xero
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create a Custom Connection app at https://developer.xero.com/app/manage with the accounting scopes you need.

mcp_servers.name: CoinGecko
mcp_servers.description: CoinGecko's official MCP server: crypto prices, market data, OHLC charts, and metadata for 15,000+ coins across 1,000+ exchanges — public tier needs no key.
mcp_servers.author: CoinGecko
mcp_servers.setup_note: Public endpoint with no key at basic rate limits; paid keys unlock the authenticated endpoint (see docs).

mcp_servers.name: Hugging Face
mcp_servers.description: Hugging Face's official MCP server: search models, datasets, Spaces, and papers on the Hub, and run your configured Gradio Spaces as tools.
mcp_servers.author: Hugging Face
mcp_servers.setup_note: Works anonymously for public content; set the Authorization header to: Bearer hf_YOUR_TOKEN (from https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens) for gated/private access and your personal tool setup.

mcp_servers.name: ElevenLabs
mcp_servers.description: ElevenLabs' official MCP server: text-to-speech, voice cloning, transcription, and audio transformation via the ElevenLabs API.
mcp_servers.author: ElevenLabs
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Get an API key at https://elevenlabs.io/app/settings/api-keys (free tier available).

mcp_servers.name: Mapbox
mcp_servers.description: Mapbox's official MCP server: geocoding, points of interest, directions, travel-time matrices, and static map images.
mcp_servers.author: Mapbox
mcp_servers.setup_note: Get an access token at https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/.

mcp_servers.name: Google Maps
mcp_servers.description: Google Maps tools over MCP: geocoding, reverse geocoding, place search and details, distance matrix, elevation, and directions.
mcp_servers.author: Anthropic
mcp_servers.setup_note: Create an API key in the Google Cloud console with the Maps APIs enabled. (Archived reference server — still functional.)

mcp_servers.name: Auth0
mcp_servers.description: Auth0's official MCP server: manage your Auth0 tenant — applications, APIs, actions, forms, and logs — in natural language.
mcp_servers.author: Auth0
mcp_servers.setup_note: Initialize once with: npx @auth0/auth0-mcp-server init (device-flow login to your tenant). Requires an OS keychain to store credentials (libsecret/gnome-keyring on Linux).

mcp_servers.name: Snyk
mcp_servers.description: Snyk's official MCP server: scan projects for vulnerabilities — open source dependencies (SCA), code (SAST), containers, and IaC.
mcp_servers.author: Snyk
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires the Snyk CLI v1.1296+ installed and authenticated (snyk auth).

mcp_servers.name: arXiv
mcp_servers.description: Search and read arXiv papers over MCP: query by topic or author, download papers, and read them as markdown with local caching.
mcp_servers.author: Joseph Blazick
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Optionally add --storage-path /path/to/paper/storage.

mcp_servers.name: YouTube Transcript
mcp_servers.description: Fetch transcripts and captions for YouTube videos over MCP, with language selection — no API key required.
mcp_servers.author: Junpei Kawamoto
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv.

mcp_servers.name: Airbnb
mcp_servers.description: Search Airbnb listings over MCP: availability, pricing, and listing details — no API key required.
mcp_servers.author: OpenBnB
mcp_servers.setup_note: Add --ignore-robots-txt only if you accept the implications.

mcp_servers.name: Oxylabs
mcp_servers.description: Oxylabs' official MCP server: scrape websites via the Oxylabs Web Scraper API — JavaScript rendering, geo-targeting, and markdown output.
mcp_servers.author: Oxylabs
mcp_servers.setup_note: Requires Python/uv. Get Web Scraper API credentials at https://dashboard.oxylabs.io.

mcp_servers.name: Wix
mcp_servers.description: Wix's official MCP server: manage your Wix sites and business — content, bookings, e-commerce, payments, and site data.
mcp_servers.author: Wix
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Wix account on first connect.

mcp_servers.name: Close CRM
mcp_servers.description: Close's official MCP server: search and manage leads, contacts, opportunities, and activities in your Close CRM.
mcp_servers.author: Close
mcp_servers.setup_note: Authenticates with OAuth against your Close organization on first connect.
